title: Apply for a CARICOM certificate of origin
description: Get a certificate showing that your goods qualify for CARICOM Single Market preferential treatment.
summary: If you are exporting goods from Barbados to another CARICOM country and you want them to qualify for preferential customs treatment, you need a CARICOM Certificate of Origin. The certificate is the proof that the goods are eligible.

It is a combined document: part declaration by the exporter, and part certificate of origin issued by the Barbados Investment and Development Corporation (BIDC). You complete your side, BIDC checks the goods meet CARICOM rules of origin, and they issue the certified version.

Bring the commercial invoice, the bill of lading or airway bill, the packing list and any documents that show the goods meet CARICOM rules of origin. ‘Rules of origin’ usually means a minimum amount of Barbadian or CARICOM content in what you are exporting.

Without the certificate, your goods may still be exported, but they will face full customs duty in the destination country instead of the preferential rate. For most CARICOM exporters, the certificate is worth getting.

items: the country of issue, origin and destination
transport information (vessel, aircraft, place of loading)
details of the goods (marks, numbers, packages, description)
the HS tariff number, gross weight and value

items.name: Bill of lading or airway bill
Proof that the goods meet CARICOM rules of origin

how_long: Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed CARICOM Common Market combined declaration by exporter and certificate of origin to BIDC at Pelican House.

contact.address:
Pelican House, Princess Alice Highway
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days’ is an estimate.
Fees are not named – we have asked the reader to confirm with BIDC because the form does not state them.
Document list (invoice, bill of lading, packing list, rules of origin evidence) is general practice for certificates of origin.
